description_long: This is a classical-style marble sculpture depicting a seated, headless female figure draped in a flowing garment. The figure sits on a textured, rock-like base, with one leg bent and the other extended forward, revealing detailed anatomical features and folds in the fabric. The right arm rests on the rocky support, while the left arm is broken off at the elbow. The back of the sculpture is smooth, with visible musculature and the garment cascading down. The surface shows a naturalistic treatment of the human form, with careful attention to the contours of the body and the drapery. The coloration is predominantly a pale, warm-toned stone, with some areas showing subtle variations due to material aging or patina.
description: A headless, seated female figure in marble, draped in flowing fabric and resting on a rocky base. The sculpture features detailed anatomy and classical styling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the figure's torso, bent leg, and extended foot, with detailed drapery and the right arm resting on the rock.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the figure, the bent leg, and the arm resting on the textured base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth back, draped garment, and the rocky support structure.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the extended leg, the broken left arm, and the garment's folds cascading down the side.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
